extra.reasoning: The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) is VIOLATED. The transition [t_resume_glitch] (on send_attempt if (opted_out and ticks >= 5): sent := true) sets sent true while opted_out remains true, directly contradicting the intent. Counterexample (7 events, within the 9-event bound): (1) optout sets opted_out=true, sent=false, ticks=0; opted_out is never cleared by any transition. (2-6) five tick events raise ticks to 5 (capped) while keeping sent=false. (7) send_attempt now has both [t_send_blocked] (guard opted_out) and [t_resume_glitch] (guard opted_out and ticks>=5) enabled; taking the enabled glitch transition sets sent:=true while opted_out is still true. The resulting reachable state has opted_out=true and sent=true, falsifying (opted_out implies not sent). Because t_resume_glitch is a genuinely enabled transition with no stated priority overriding it, this execution is reachable, so the global invariant fails.
